verses.text: 'Blessed be the LORD my strength, which teacheth my hands to war,' David's fortress, shield, and deliverer. He owns God as the source of his skill and safety. Every ability and defence the believer has is a gift from God.

'Blessed be the LORD my strength, which teacheth my hands to war,' David's fortress, shield, and deliverer. He owns God as the source of his skill and safety. Every ability and defence the believer has is a gift from God.

He marvels, 'LORD, what is man, that thou takest knowledge of him?' for man is like a breath, his days a passing shadow. He sees human frailty. That the great God should mind such fleeting creatures is the wonder of His grace.

He marvels, 'LORD, what is man, that thou takest knowledge of him?' for man is like a breath, his days a passing shadow. He sees human frailty. That the great God should mind such fleeting creatures is the wonder of His grace.

He prays God to 'bow thy heavens... and come down,' to scatter his foes and deliver him from lying enemies. He calls for God's mighty intervention. When men's hands are false, the believer cries for the hand of God.

He prays God to 'bow thy heavens... and come down,' to scatter his foes and deliver him from lying enemies. He calls for God's mighty intervention. When men's hands are false, the believer cries for the hand of God.

He prays God to 'bow thy heavens... and come down,' to scatter his foes and deliver him from lying enemies. He calls for God's mighty intervention. When men's hands are false, the believer cries for the hand of God.
He vows a new song to the God 'that giveth salvation unto kings,' asking again to be rescued from deceitful foes. Praise and prayer mingle. The rescued heart sings even while it still pleads.
He vows a new song to the God 'that giveth salvation unto kings,' asking again to be rescued from deceitful foes. Praise and prayer mingle. The rescued heart sings even while it still pleads.
He vows a new song to the God 'that giveth salvation unto kings,' asking again to be rescued from deceitful foes. Praise and prayer mingle. The rescued heart sings even while it still pleads.

He prays for sons like grown plants, daughters like polished corners, full barns and peace, concluding, 'happy is that people, whose God is the LORD.' True blessedness is named. No prosperity compares with having the LORD as our God.
